name: St. Joseph
title: Foster Father of Jesus
short_bio: St. Joseph is the just man chosen to guard Jesus and Mary. Scripture portrays him as obedient, humble, and decisive in difficult moments.

name: St. Monica
title: Mother of St. Augustine
short_bio: St. Monica prayed for years for her son Augustine's conversion. Her perseverance and tears became a model of patient intercession.

name: St. Augustine
title: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
short_bio: After a restless youth, Augustine converted and became one of the Church's most influential theologians. His writings explore grace, desire, and conversion.

name: St. Thérèse of Lisieux
title: Doctor of the Church
short_bio: St. Thérèse taught the 'little way' of holiness through ordinary acts done with great love. Her trust in God's mercy remains globally beloved.

name: St. Ignatius of Loyola
title: Founder of the Jesuits
short_bio: A former soldier, Ignatius converted after injury and developed the Spiritual Exercises. He is known for discernment of spirits and mission.

name: St. Francis of Assisi
title: Founder of the Franciscans
short_bio: Francis embraced evangelical poverty and joyful simplicity after a profound conversion. He is remembered for peace-making and love of creation.

name: St. Thomas Aquinas
title: Doctor of the Church
short_bio: Thomas Aquinas integrated faith and reason in a lasting theological synthesis. His humility and devotion accompanied exceptional intellectual work.

name: St. Gianna Molla
title: Wife, Mother, and Physician
short_bio: Gianna Molla was an Italian doctor and mother who integrated professional service with family life. She is honored for heroic love and fidelity to life.

name: St. Dymphna
title: Virgin and Martyr
short_bio: St. Dymphna is venerated as a patron for those experiencing anxiety and mental distress. Her witness is associated with compassion and refuge.

name: St. John Vianney
title: Curé of Ars
short_bio: A parish priest known for long hours in the confessional and pastoral charity, John Vianney became a model for priestly holiness.

name: St. Faustina Kowalska
title: Apostle of Divine Mercy
short_bio: St. Faustina recorded messages emphasizing trust in Jesus' mercy. Her diary shaped modern devotion to Divine Mercy.

name: St. Padre Pio
title: Capuchin Priest
short_bio: Padre Pio was known for prayer, spiritual counsel, and love for the Eucharist. Many sought his intercession in suffering.

name: St. Elizabeth Ann Seton
title: Religious Founder
short_bio: A widow and mother who founded a teaching community, Elizabeth Ann Seton advanced Catholic education in the United States.

name: St. John Paul II
title: Pope
short_bio: John Paul II led the Church through decades of global change and repeatedly called people to be unafraid in following Christ.

name: St. Teresa of Ávila
title: Doctor of the Church
short_bio: Teresa reformed Carmelite life and wrote classics on contemplative prayer. She taught practical perseverance in friendship with God.

name: St. Benedict
title: Abbot and Patriarch of Western Monasticism
short_bio: Benedict authored a rule balancing prayer, work, and community life. His monastic wisdom shaped Christian culture across centuries.

name: St. Clare of Assisi
title: Founder of the Poor Clares
short_bio: Inspired by Francis, Clare embraced a life of poverty and contemplative prayer. She guided her sisters with humility and strength.

name: St. Maximilian Kolbe
title: Priest and Martyr
short_bio: In Auschwitz, Maximilian Kolbe offered his life in place of another prisoner. He is remembered for sacrificial love and Marian devotion.

name: St. Cecilia
title: Virgin and Martyr
short_bio: St. Cecilia is traditionally honored as patron saint of musicians. Her witness reflects steadfast faith amid hardship.

name: Bl. Carlo Acutis
title: Blessed
short_bio: Carlo Acutis used technology to share devotion to the Eucharist and lived ordinary holiness as a teenager. He is a modern witness of joyful faith.

name: St. Rita of Cascia
title: Augustinian Nun
short_bio: St. Rita endured a difficult marriage and the loss of her husband and sons before entering religious life. She is venerated as patroness of impossible causes and those carrying long-suffering grief.

name: Ven. Matt Talbot
title: Layman, Servant of God
short_bio: An Irish dockworker who recovered from severe alcoholism through prayer, penance, and the sacraments. His witness is widely invoked by those struggling with addiction and recovery.

name: Sts. Louis & Zélie Martin
title: Married Couple, Parents of St. Thérèse
short_bio: The first married couple canonized together. They lived ordinary professional and family life with extraordinary fidelity, raising five daughters who entered religious life including St. Thérèse of Lisieux.

name: St. Anne
title: Mother of the Blessed Virgin Mary
short_bio: Honored in tradition as the mother of Mary and grandmother of Jesus. She is invoked by mothers, grandmothers, and those longing for children.

name: St. John Henry Newman
title: Cardinal and Doctor of the Church
short_bio: An Anglican cleric and Oxford scholar whose long, costly conversion led him into the Catholic Church. His writings on conscience, doubt, and intellectual honesty remain widely read.

name: St. Teresa Benedicta of the Cross (Edith Stein)
title: Carmelite Nun and Martyr
short_bio: A Jewish philosopher and convert to Catholicism who entered Carmel and was killed at Auschwitz. Patron of Europe, of converts, and of those whose faith was hard-won through study.

name: St. Bernadette Soubirous
title: Visionary of Lourdes
short_bio: A poor and chronically ill girl who experienced the apparitions at Lourdes. She is a patroness of the sick and of those whose faithfulness is hidden.

name: St. John Bosco (Don Bosco)
title: Founder of the Salesians
short_bio: A priest of Turin who devoted his life to forming and protecting young people. He is widely invoked by youth, students, and educators.

name: St. Óscar Romero
title: Archbishop of San Salvador and Martyr
short_bio: An archbishop who became a tireless defender of the poor and was assassinated while celebrating Mass. His witness is invoked by those serving justice and mercy at cost.

name: St. Maria Goretti
title: Virgin and Martyr
short_bio: An Italian girl who forgave her attacker before dying of his wounds. Her witness is associated with mercy in the face of grave harm and with hope for victims and offenders alike.

name: St. Teresa of Calcutta (Mother Teresa)
title: Founder of the Missionaries of Charity
short_bio: Founded a religious community to serve the poorest of the poor in Calcutta and worldwide. Her decades of spiritual darkness, revealed posthumously, comfort those suffering dryness in faith.

name: St. Michael the Archangel
title: Archangel
short_bio: Defender against evil and protector of the faithful. The traditional St. Michael Prayer is invoked for spiritual protection.

name: Bl. Pier Giorgio Frassati
title: Lay Dominican
short_bio: An Italian student known for joyful charity, mountaineering, and quiet care for the poor. He died young of polio and is widely beloved by young adults seeking ordinary holiness.

name: St. Philip Neri
title: Founder of the Oratorians
short_bio: A Roman priest known for joyful holiness, friendship, and gentle confession. He is invoked against scrupulosity and for joyful perseverance.

name: St. Gemma Galgani
title: Mystic and Lay Person
short_bio: An Italian laywoman who endured profound suffering with mystical patience. She is a patroness for those experiencing illness and loss in young adulthood.

name: Blessed Virgin Mary
title: Mother of God
short_bio: The Mother of Jesus, honored as Theotokos and model of perfect discipleship. Her 'fiat' is the pattern of obedient faith for every Christian.

name: St. Peter the Apostle
title: First Pope and Apostle
short_bio: A Galilean fisherman called by Jesus to be the rock of the Church. Despite denying Christ, he was restored and led the early Church to martyrdom in Rome.

name: St. Paul the Apostle
title: Apostle to the Gentiles
short_bio: A persecutor of Christians transformed by an encounter with the risen Christ. His letters and missionary journeys shaped the early Church and Christian theology.

name: St. John the Apostle
title: Beloved Disciple and Evangelist
short_bio: The beloved disciple, author of the fourth Gospel and the only apostle present at the Cross. Entrusted with Mary, he is a witness to divine love.

name: St. James the Greater
title: Apostle and Martyr
short_bio: Brother of John and the first apostle to be martyred. His shrine at Compostela has drawn pilgrims for over a millennium.

name: St. Andrew the Apostle
title: Apostle and Martyr
short_bio: Brother of Peter and the first-called of the apostles. He preached widely and was crucified on an X-shaped cross.

name: St. John the Baptist
title: Forerunner of Christ
short_bio: The prophet who prepared the way for Jesus and baptized him in the Jordan. He bore witness with his life and his death.

name: St. Joachim
title: Father of the Blessed Virgin Mary
short_bio: Honored in tradition as the husband of St. Anne and father of Mary. He represents the faithful grandfathers and quiet patriarchs of the family.

name: St. Jerome
title: Doctor of the Church and Scripture Scholar
short_bio: Translator of the Latin Vulgate and a fiery defender of orthodoxy. His ascetic life in Bethlehem produced enduring scholarship on Scripture.

name: St. Ambrose
title: Bishop of Milan and Doctor of the Church
short_bio: Bishop of Milan whose preaching helped convert Augustine. He defended the Church against imperial overreach and shaped Western liturgy.

name: St. Gregory the Great
title: Pope and Doctor of the Church
short_bio: A Roman aristocrat who became pope during plague and political collapse. He shepherded the Church, reformed liturgy, and sent missions to England.

name: St. Basil the Great
title: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
short_bio: A Cappadocian Father who organized monastic life in the East and championed orthodox Trinitarian faith. His charity to the poor was legendary.

name: St. John Chrysostom
title: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
short_bio: Patriarch of Constantinople renowned for golden-mouthed preaching and defense of the poor. He was exiled for confronting injustice in high places.

name: St. Catherine of Siena
title: Doctor of the Church and Mystic
short_bio: A Dominican tertiary and mystic who counseled popes and cared for the sick. Her Dialogue is a classic of mystical theology.

name: St. Bonaventure
title: Doctor of the Church
short_bio: A Franciscan friar and theologian who guided his order and wrote on the soul's journey to God. He is called the Seraphic Doctor.

name: St. John of the Cross
title: Doctor of the Church and Mystic
short_bio: A Carmelite reformer with Teresa of Ávila and poet of the dark night of the soul. He guides those passing through spiritual aridity.

name: St. Hildegard of Bingen
title: Doctor of the Church and Mystic
short_bio: A Benedictine abbess, visionary, composer, and natural scientist. She wrote on cosmology, medicine, and the wisdom of God in creation.

name: St. Dominic
title: Founder of the Order of Preachers
short_bio: Founder of the Dominicans, devoted to preaching truth with charity. His order has shaped theology and pastoral life for eight centuries.

name: St. Francis Xavier
title: Missionary and Co-founder of the Jesuits
short_bio: A Jesuit missionary who brought the Gospel across India, Japan, and Southeast Asia. He is patron of all foreign missions.

name: St. Patrick
title: Bishop and Missionary to Ireland
short_bio: Brought to Ireland as a slave, he escaped and returned as a missionary bishop. His Breastplate prayer remains a classic of Christian devotion.

name: St. Boniface
title: Apostle of Germany and Martyr
short_bio: An English monk who evangelized Germanic peoples and reformed the Frankish Church. He was martyred preaching to those he served.

name: St. Junípero Serra
title: Missionary and Franciscan Friar
short_bio: A Spanish Franciscan who founded the California missions. He is honored for tireless missionary zeal across the American Southwest.

name: St. Damien of Molokai
title: Priest and Missionary to Lepers
short_bio: A Belgian priest who served lepers in Hawaii and eventually died of the disease himself. He embodies the courage of presence with the suffering.

name: St. Frances Xavier Cabrini
title: Founder and Missionary
short_bio: Founded the Missionary Sisters of the Sacred Heart and served Italian immigrants in the Americas. First U.S. citizen canonized.

name: St. Stephen
title: Protomartyr and Deacon
short_bio: The first Christian martyr and one of the seven deacons. He died forgiving his attackers, mirroring his Lord on the cross.

name: St. Lawrence
title: Deacon and Martyr
short_bio: A Roman deacon martyred for safeguarding the Church's treasure: the poor. His joyful courage on the gridiron became legendary.

name: St. Agnes
title: Virgin and Martyr
short_bio: A young Roman girl martyred for her faith and her vow of chastity. She has been honored since the earliest centuries of the Church.

name: St. Sebastian
title: Martyr
short_bio: A Roman soldier martyred for his faith and famously depicted pierced with arrows. Invoked against plague and for athletes.

name: St. Lucy
title: Virgin and Martyr
short_bio: A young martyr of Syracuse whose name means light. She is invoked for eye troubles and for finding light in darkness.

name: St. Joan of Arc
title: Virgin and Martyr
short_bio: A French peasant girl who led armies and was burned at the stake at age nineteen. She is patron of soldiers and of those misjudged by authorities.

name: St. Thomas More
title: Lord Chancellor and Martyr
short_bio: An English statesman and humanist scholar martyred for refusing Henry VIII's break with Rome. He is patron of statesmen, lawyers, and those navigating conscience at work.

name: St. Catherine of Genoa
title: Mystic and Hospital Reformer
short_bio: A married laywoman and mystic who served plague victims and wrote on purgation and divine love. She integrated contemplation and care of the sick.

name: St. Margaret Mary Alacoque
title: Mystic and Visitation Nun
short_bio: A Visitation nun whose visions of the Sacred Heart sparked one of the most enduring devotions in the Church. She suffered greatly in obedience to her mission.

name: St. Vincent de Paul
title: Founder of the Vincentians
short_bio: A French priest who organized lay charity at unprecedented scale, founding congregations to serve the poor, the sick, and orphans.

name: St. Katharine Drexel
title: Heiress and Founder
short_bio: An American heiress who gave away a vast fortune to found schools and missions for Native and Black Americans. She is patron of racial justice in education.

name: St. Isidore the Farmer
title: Layman and Laborer
short_bio: A Spanish day laborer who sanctified ordinary fieldwork with continual prayer. He and his wife Maria are patrons of farmers and rural life.

name: St. Jude Thaddeus
title: Apostle
short_bio: One of the Twelve Apostles, traditionally invoked as patron of desperate or seemingly hopeless causes. His epistle exhorts perseverance in faith.

name: St. Anthony of Padua
title: Doctor of the Church and Franciscan Preacher
short_bio: A Portuguese-born Franciscan whose preaching converted many. He is universally invoked for lost items and for finding what truly matters.

name: St. Christopher
title: Martyr
short_bio: Tradition portrays him as a giant who carried the Christ Child across a river. He is widely invoked for protection in travel and transitions.

name: St. Elizabeth of Hungary
title: Princess and Franciscan Tertiary
short_bio: A young princess and widow who poured out her wealth on the poor and sick. She is patron of those balancing family duty with radical charity.

name: St. Helena
title: Empress and Mother of Constantine
short_bio: Mother of Emperor Constantine, she made pilgrimage to the Holy Land and is honored for finding the True Cross. She is patron of converts and difficult marriages.

name: St. Gabriel the Archangel
title: Archangel of the Annunciation
short_bio: The messenger who announced the births of John the Baptist and Jesus. He is invoked by communicators and those waiting for clarity.

name: St. Raphael the Archangel
title: Archangel of Healing
short_bio: The archangel who guided Tobiah in the Book of Tobit and brought healing. He is invoked for healing, travel, and matchmaking.

name: St. John XXIII
title: Pope
short_bio: The 'good pope' who convoked the Second Vatican Council and was beloved for his warmth. His simplicity and patience opened the Church to dialogue with the modern world.

name: St. Pius X
title: Pope
short_bio: A pope of humble peasant origins who reformed liturgy, music, and catechesis. He encouraged frequent and early Holy Communion.

name: St. Josephine Bakhita
title: Religious Sister and Former Slave
short_bio: Born in Sudan and sold into slavery as a child, she gained freedom in Italy and entered religious life. Patron of survivors of trafficking and unjust suffering.

name: St. André Bessette
title: Religious Brother
short_bio: A frail Quebec porter and brother of the Holy Cross whose devotion to St. Joseph drew thousands seeking healing. He embodied hidden, humble service.

name: St. Juan Diego
title: Visionary of Guadalupe
short_bio: An indigenous Mexican man who received the apparitions of Our Lady of Guadalupe in 1531. His tilma bearing her image continues to draw pilgrims.

name: St. Rose of Lima
title: Dominican Tertiary and Mystic
short_bio: The first canonized saint of the Americas, a Peruvian laywoman who lived a hidden life of penance and care for the poor and indigenous.

name: St. Martin de Porres
title: Dominican Brother
short_bio: A Peruvian Dominican brother of mixed race who served the sick, animals, and the marginalized with humility. Patron of social justice and interracial harmony.

name: St. Kateri Tekakwitha
title: Lily of the Mohawks
short_bio: A Mohawk-Algonquin convert who lived a brief life of prayer and penance. The first Native American canonized saint.

name: St. Josemaría Escrivá
title: Founder of Opus Dei
short_bio: A Spanish priest who taught that ordinary work, lived with love, is a path to holiness. He founded Opus Dei to promote sanctification in daily life.
